notes: Theory-only multi-theory batch demo over 5 standardized temporal Hawkes theories; NO simulator and NO sim/theory overlay (the comparison plot is theory-vs-theory, not theory-vs-sim). Temporal (no Laplacian/spatial block); multi-field/multi-population: each theory is a 2-population Hawkes model with nt/vt response fields and dn/dv physical fields. CONTRACT MISMATCH blocking a clean migration: the 5 theories live as Python PACKAGE modules under pipeline/theories/*.py (linear_hawkes_2pop_delta, linear_hawkes_2pop_expg, quad_hawkes_2pop_expg, linear_hawkes_2pop_expg_gtas, quad_hawkes_2pop_expg_gtas), imported via importlib.import_module and read off mod.HAWKES_MODEL (a prebuilt dict). They build inline via pipeline.theory.TheoryBuilder + pipeline.theory_templates (HawkesAction, ExpSynapticKernel, GTaSNoise). nb_support.load_theory(name) instead reads theories/<name>.theory.py and calls mod.b

Runtime dominated by the sim: T_sim=2e6, dt_sim=0.01, N_RUNS=4 numba runs (JIT warmup in cell 11 first). Theory side is cheap (max_ell=0, algebraic equations so DAE runs stability_analysis=False). PRESERVE: (1) the multi-field stacked-counts overlay — somatic+dendritic spike counts stacked via stack_binned_counts so one cumulant_estimator handles either leg; pop_indices come from flat_index_of(stack_offsets,...) using field_types passed to compute_kpoint_slice. (2) CAP_PD_AT_ONE flag threaded into the simulator (p_D cap at 1 = paper convention, the known theory/sim mismatch source). (3) the rate/Bernoulli-prob diagnostics in the top three panels (sim rates totS/totD/T_sim and pD_b.mean vs MF saddle predictions incl. linear pre-cap pD_pred). (4) BASE_SEED from secrets.randbits(31) + per-run offset. theory_runner can supply model/cfg/mod; the sim block (cells 10-12) and 2x2 plot (cell 14)

notes: DUAL-THEORY notebook: loads TWO theory files in one cell (cell 5) via a local _load_theory helper -> single_population_linear_delta_voltage_test AND single_population_linear_delta_spikes_test. It calls compute_cumulants TWICE (cell 7, _run helper, once per model) and the CORE diagnostic (cell 9, theory-vs-theory equality check) asserts the two formulations give bit-identical n-correlators (max|voltage-spikes|=0.000e+00). This voltage-vs-spikes equivalence test is the notebook's scientific point and is NOT expressible in nb_support's single-model run()/plot_cumulant() API, which assumes ONE model. Migrating would require either two separate nb_support runs plus a bespoke cross-comparison cell, or an API extension; either way the validated diagnostic + 3-way plot must be preserved by hand. Multi-field/multi-pop: N=2 populations (pops sizes summed), so group=temporal-multi. Runtime: theory

Inline build via legacy TheoryBuilder('allen-cahn-1d', n_populations=0) with .physical_field('phi', spatial_dim=1), params mu=1,D=1,lam=0.1,T=1, action 'phit*((Dt + mu - D*Laplacian)*phi + lam*phi^3) - T*phit^2', boundary infinite / initial stationary. An EXACT-equivalent theory file ALREADY EXISTS: theories/allen_cahn_1d_subcritical_infinite.theory.py (uses SpatialTheoryBuilder; identical action/equation/params, DEFAULT_FUNDAMENTAL + METADATA k_default=2, ell_default=0, spatial_grid). So migration = swap inline allen_cahn() for nb_support.load_theory('allen_cahn_1d_subcritical_infinite'); note that theory's recommended_external_fields=[('phi',1),('phi',2)] differs from the notebook's [('phi',1),('phi',1)] -- the notebook's k=2 equal-time variance setup must be preserved via Config(external_fields). Loads theory via a local exec helper `load_theory(path)` reading a HARDCODED relative path '../theories/allen_cahn_1d_subcritical_infinite.theory.py' (the model['name'] confirms 1D stochastic Allen-Cahn subcritical infinite) — migrate to nb_support.load_theory('allen_cahn_1d_subcritical_infinite'). model is a dict (SpatialTheoryBuilder build) with model['spatial']['dim']=1; subcritical saddle phistar=0. compute_cumulants called with parallel=False, use_cache=False, k=2, max_ell in {0,1}, x_grid=linspace(0,5,26), tau_max=3, tau_step=0.25; spatial_info dict exposes self_energy_coeff_g (=3*lam tadpole), g_q_spread, phi2_0, Sigma, A_eff_hartree — these per-diagram/self-energy diagnostics (cell 6 prints, cell 11 table) are validated and MUST be preserved; nb_support's plot_cumulant likely won't reproduce the bespoke tree/1-loop/Hartree/sim overlay or the lambda-sweep, so keep cells 6, 11, 12

Spatial single-field phi^6 (Allen-Cahn + quintic) generalization test. Builds model INLINE via TheoryBuilder('allen-cahn-quintic-1d') with set_action_text 'phit*((Dt + mu - D*Laplacian)*phi + lam*phi^3 + gamma*phi^5) - T*phit^2'; a matching canonical theory file theories/allen_cahn_quintic_1d_subcritical_infinite.theory.py ALREADY EXISTS and is structurally identical (uses SpatialTheoryBuilder + stability_analysis(True)), so migration is a clean theory-file swap rather than a new extraction. NOTE: inline builder uses TheoryBuilder (not SpatialTheoryBuilder) and omits stability_analysis(True) that the theory file has — preserve the theory-file form. Params mu=D=T=1.0, lam=gamma=0.1. Cell 5 loops compute_cumulants over ell=0,1,2 (equal-time tau_max=0.0, spatial_grid xs=linspace(0,6,25), use_cache=False, mf_dae_n_starts=4, verbose=True), keeping the tau=0 row of C_tau_x; this is the load-be

Spatial single-field d=1 damped Burgers, k=2 two-point equal-time (tau_max=0) correlator. Notebook builds the model INLINE via pipeline.theory.TheoryBuilder('burgers-1d', n_populations=0) in cell 1's build_theory(), re-called fresh per order in cell 5 (use_cache=False, mf_dae_n_starts=4). A matching theory file ALREADY EXISTS at theories/burgers_1d.theory.py with bit-identical field/params/equation/action text (uses SpatialTheoryBuilder vs the notebook's TheoryBuilder; DEFAULT lam=0.3 same) -> nb_support.load_theory('burgers_1d') is a drop-in, no new extraction needed. fundamental = {mu:1.0, D:1.0, lam:0.3, T:1.0}. K_EXTERNAL!=2 raises NotImplementedError (k=2-gated). MAX_ELL=1 default; markdown notes ell=2 correct but slow. Vertex is composite first-derivative Dx(phi^2,0): imaginary form factor (Dx->ik), real part at output; bilinear drift cross-term vanishes at saddle phi*=0. No Dyson

Inline build_combined() in cell 1 uses TheoryBuilder('allen-cahn+modelB+kpz-1d', n_populations=0).physical_field('phi', spatial_dim=1) with mu,D,lam,g,kpz,T params, equation (Dt+mu-D*Laplacian)*phi = -lam*phi^3, and set_action_text combining lam*phi^3 (Allen-Cahn) - g*Lap(phi^2) (Model B) - (kpz/2)*Dx(phi,0)^2 (KPZ) - T*phit^2, then .operator_ir().boundary('infinite').initial('stationary'). Migration requires extracting a theories/<name>.theory.py first (no combined theory file exists; theories/ only has separate allen_cahn_1d_subcritical_infinite, allen_cahn_quintic, kpz_1d). PHYSICS CAVEAT to preserve: Model B x KPZ cross bubble is UV-divergent in d=1 (degree-4 loop form factor) so the bare combined 1-loop C(0,0) can go NEGATIVE -- this is a documented real feature, not a bug; the cell-5 try/except prints a note when C(0,0)<0 and the markdown extensively explains it. Sim is the trusted

Combined 3-vertex spatial SPDE at d=2 (single physical field 'phi', spatial_dim=2): Allen-Cahn phi^3 + Model B g*Lap(phi^2) + KPZ (kpz/2)((Dx phi,0)^2+(Dx phi,1)^2). Built INLINE via TheoryBuilder('allen-cahn+modelB+kpz-2d', n_populations=0).physical_field('phi', spatial_dim=2)...set_action_text(...).operator_ir().boundary('infinite').initial('stationary').build() in cell 1's build_combined(). NO matching theory file exists (theories/ has only allen_cahn_1d_* and kpz_1d, all d=1) -> needs_theory_extraction: must author a new theories/allen_cahn_modelb_kpz_2d.theory.py to migrate. Params: mu=1,D=1,lam=0.1,g=0.1,kpz=0.2,T=1.0. compute_cumulants called in cell 5 in a loop over orders=range(0,MAX_ELL+1) (MAX_ELL=1) inside try/except theory_ok guard; reads out['C_tau_x'], takes middle tau row (mid=shape[0]//2), curve=real(...)[mid]; kw passes spatial_grid=xs (np.linspace(0,5,15)), tau_max=0.0

Inline build via TheoryBuilder in cell 1 (build_combined()), rebuilt fresh inside the compute loop each order. d=3 single-field combined model: Allen-Cahn phi^3 + Model B g*Lap(phi^2) + KPZ (kpz/2)*sum_i Dx(phi,i)^2, all three axes (i=0,1,2). Uses .operator_ir() authoring path, boundary('infinite'), initial('stationary'), n_populations=0. Compute call (cell 5) uses compute_cumulants with spatial_grid=xs (13 radial pts 0..5), tau_max=0.0/tau_step=1.0 (equal-time), use_cache=False, mf_dae_n_starts=4, verbose=False; loops orders 0..MAX_ELL (MAX_ELL=1). C(r,0) taken as np.real(out['C_tau_x'])[mid] (mid time-row). Params: mu=D=T=1.0, lam=0.1, g=0.1, kpz=0.2. d=3 1-loop is UV-DIVERGENT (same-signature Model B x KPZ cross) by design -- the notebook deliberately only plots tree vs sim and reports the (off-scale) 1-loop in text; PRESERVE that the demo's claim is 'it composes/computes', NOT theory

notes:
Spatial (Laplacian, d=1), TWO physical fields a,b with non-normal reaction matrix M=[[mua,g],[-h,mub]] (complex eigenpair ~1.35+-0.31i -> damped-oscillatory, tau-ASYMMETRIC C_ab). Builds the model INLINE via a local `coupled_rd(Da,Db,ga_,gb_,dyson,periodic_L)` factory using SpatialTheoryBuilder (cell 1) — does NOT import/exec a theory file. A MATCHING theory file ALREADY EXISTS: theories/coupled_rd_2species_1d.theory.py (build() reproduces the default equal-D Da=Db=0.8, ga=gb=0.3, infinite boundary case bit-for-bit; DEFAULT_FUNDAMENTAL + METADATA present). So the default-config theory core is extractable/loadable today; the friction is the notebook's PARAMETRIC rebuilds.

Three distinct compute_cumulants regimes, all reachable through nb_support.Config but NOT from a single static load:

Migration = load_theory('kpz_1d') instead of inline build; no extraction needed, hence clean. PRESERVE: (1) the per-leg 'perleg' vertex mode is the whole point of this notebook (the (∂h)² != ∂(h²) KPZ signature) — verify the loaded theory still reports vertex_mo

sim: models.spatial_field_1d_sim :: simulate, equal_time_correlator, lattice_sum_variance, plus an inline temporal_autocorr() defined in the notebook
notes: Loads theory file via hardcoded path '../theories/linear_diffusion_test.theory.py' with a local load_theory() helper that calls mod.build() — maps cleanly onto nb_support.load_theory('linear_diffusion_test'). Linear/Gaussian theory so max_ell=0 tree-level is EXACT (framework vs closed form ~1e-16); fast runtime, no loop integration. compute_cumulants(cell 6) requests BOTH spatial_grid (x_grid=linspace(0,6,25)) and tau grid (tau_max=4.0, tau_step=0.25) in one call, reading the tau=0 row for C(x,0) and the x=0 column for C(0,tau) from C_tau_x of shape (n_tau,n_x) — the shared run()/plot_cumulant must support a combined spatial+tau slice extraction. PARAMS={mu:1,D:1,T:1} (fundamental). Simulator (cell 8) is the main runtime cost: simulate(L=20,N=200,n_steps=200_000,burn_in=20_000,record_every=5,seed=7) — spectral exponential integrator, lam=0.0; preserve seed=7 and these run-length params f

Tree-level (max_ell=0) d=2 spatial path; single physical field 'phi' with spatial_dim=2, n_populations=0. Config to preserve: k=2, max_ell=0, external_fields=[('phi',1),('phi',1)], spatial_grid=rs=linspace(0.3,4.0,16), tau_max=2.0, tau_step=0.5, use_cache=False, mf_dae_n_starts=4. sim: models.spatial_field_2d_sim :: simulate_2d, radial_structure_factor_2d (also imports radial_correlator_2d/structure_factor_2d via module but only these two called)
notes: Inline 2-D reaction-diffusion field theory dphi=D*Lap*phi - mu*phi - g*phi^2 + eta built via TheoryBuilder as a g-parameterized factory function reaction_diffusion_2d(g) (n_populations=0, single physical_field 'phi' spatial_dim=2). To migrate, extract a theory file like reaction_diffusion_quadratic_2d.theory.py (a 2-D analog of the existing theories/reaction_diffusion_quadratic_1d.theory.py) -- but note g is a free parameter swept inside the notebook (g=0.2, then g2=0.4), and the loop g^2-scaling diagnostic (cell 6) REBUILDS the model at a second coupling and divides corrections; this re-parameterization must be preserved by passing g via Config.fundamental at each call rather than baking it into the builder. Tree is g-independent (phi*=0) so g only enters at ell>=1. compute_cumulants called at TWO orders (max_ell=0 tree in cell 3, max_ell=1 loop in cells 3 and 6); shared kwargs k=2, ext

Spatial single-field 'Conserved Model B': dphi/dt = D d2phi - mu phi + g d2(phi^2) + eta, composite-derivative (Lap) vertex with form factors F_R=q^2 l^2, F_K=q^4; conservation forces Sigma(q->0)->0. 11 cells (6 markdown, 5 code). Cell 1 imports + defines build_theory() via pipeline.theory.TheoryBuilder (NOT SpatialTheoryBuilder) with .operator_ir(); params mu=1,D=2,g=0.25,T=1. Cell 3 sets MAX_ELL=1 (loop knob; ell=2 noted correct-but-slow), K_EXTERNAL=2 (raises NotImplementedError if !=2), kw dict (external_fields=[('phi',1),('phi',1)], spatial_grid=xs=linspace(0,6,25), tau_max=0.0/tau_step=1.0 => equal-time only, verbose, use_cache=False, mf_dae_n_starts=4) and fund dict. Cell 5 loops ell in 0..MAX_ELL calling compute_cumulants(build_theory(), max_ell=ell, fundamental=fund, **kw), takes tau=0 row (mid=shape[0]//2) of real(C_tau_x); prints vertex_mode/n_live_diagrams from spatial_info +
